cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what we learn about the Puya chilensis in England from the passage. The options are A, B, C, or D. First, I need to go back through the text and pick out relevant information. The passage mentions that the Royal Horticultural Society (RHS) has been feeding the plant liquid fertilizer. The quote says, "We keep it well fed with liquid fertilizer as feeding it on its natural diet might prove a bit problematic," according to Cara Smith. So that seems to directly support option A, that it's fed man-made liquid fertilizer there. Option B says it often kills sheep. But the passage states that in its natural habitat, it's believed to trap sheep, but in England, they're using liquid fertilizer instead. Also, the text says "despite its frightening description... is considered a threatened species." Also, in the RHS greenhouse, they mention the spikes are out of reach of both children and sheep. So in England, it's not killing sheep because they're not allowing animals near it, and they're feeding it with liquid fertilizer. So B is probably incorrect. Option C says it has once bloomed 15 years before. The passage says the plant was planted 15 years ago, and now it's about to bloom for the first time after more than a decade of cultivation. So 15 years ago was when it was planted, but it hasn't bloomed until now. So C is incorrect. Option D says it uses animals' rotting bodies as fertilizer.
